_note_on_prior_content: This file was previously occupied by a stale D+H bugfix BA context from a parallel cycle (Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715). That cycle's task-id collided with this one. The D+H context has been superseded by the harness work described below, which is the authoritative BA context for task-id 20260519-211515 per the user requirement document at docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260519-211515.md.
_risk_level_doc: high — modifies shared infrastructure (score-update.sh + .gitignore + commands/close.md + commands/commit.md + commands/dev.md + agents/qa.md) that all downstream cycles depend on. Bounded blast radius: gate is a NEW pre-debate insertion in /close (Step 1.5) and /commit PRIMARY path; sandbox harnesses are NEW additive scripts; CAS+log-emission in score-update.sh is backward-compat-preserving (optional flag). Test-writer dispatch gate fires per commands/dev.md Step 8 (complexity_tier=STANDARD); acceptance_criteria_path below points to non-null AC JSON.

requirement.clarified: Implement all 3 TOP clusters (8 of 17 systemic issues) from docs/dev/meta-assessment-20260519-161035.json per user-requirement-dev-20260519-211515.md: (Cluster 1, AC1-AC5) shippability gate detecting gitignored / out-of-repo / ephemeral-mount artifacts in dev outputs; (Cluster 2, AC6-AC8) real-fixture sandboxed verification harness replacing --dry-run + PATH-shim mocks; (Cluster 3, AC9-AC13) event-sourced lifecycle log at logs/lifecycle.jsonl with CAS on score-update transitions plus orchestrator quota-wall + resumption events.
requirement.what: Create 6 new scripts (shippability-gate.py, sandbox-destructive.sh, sandbox-pressure.sh, sandbox-common.sh, lifecycle-log.sh, tmp-cleanup-install.sh) and modify 6 files (score-update.sh, close.md, commit.md, dev.md, qa.md, .gitignore) to land all 13 ACs.
requirement.why: Prior cycle 20260519-161035 (4-layer tmpfs prevention) shipped artifacts that were gitignored (docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md), out-of-repo (/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh), and on ephemeral mounts (/tmp/claude-pressure-warn-*); /close said YES; the deliverable was broken on origin/master. This cycle is the systemic backfill that prevents recurrence.
success_criteria: AC1: Gate fails closed on gitignored entry without waiver
AC2: Gate fails closed on out-of-repo path without system_file_waiver
AC3: Gate fails closed on ephemeral persistence without enospc_fallback_path
AC4: Gate applied to prior cycle 20260519-161035 dev-report (with persistent_state_files[] augmentation) fires all 3 reasons
AC5: Gate wiring is automatic in /close Step 1.5 — no manual orchestrator action
AC6: sandbox-destructive.sh exercises real script under mount-namespace remap with correct delete/preserve assertions
AC7: sandbox-pressure.sh manufactures real >75% mount and captures real hook output
AC8: agents/qa.md documents both harnesses + honors requires_fixture_verification flag
AC9: logs/lifecycle.jsonl receives valid JSONL with {ts, dev_session_id, event_type, agent, payload}
AC10: score-update.sh emits lifecycle entry on every score change with full payload
AC11: score-update.sh CAS race: concurrent calls — one succeeds, one fails with CAS_CONFLICT exit 3
AC12: Orchestrator quota-wall event writable via lifecycle-log.sh, documented in commands/dev.md
AC13: Orchestrator resumption event writable via lifecycle-log.sh, documented in commands/dev.md
constraints: All deliverables in-repo and not gitignored (gate dogfoods itself on this cycle's dev-report)
Existing score-update.sh callers MUST work unchanged (backward-compat binding)
Existing 4-layer tmpfs hooks (session-tmpfs-banner.sh, userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh, tmp-cleanup.sh) MUST NOT change behavior
Lifecycle log path: logs/lifecycle.jsonl (this repo's runtime path; user-text says .claude/logs/lifecycle.jsonl — multi-authority conflict resolved per ticket Reference Source section)
No new daemons / Prometheus / monitoring stacks (additive only)
unshare -m may be unavailable; harness must exit capability_unavailable=2 (NOT silent PASS)
Sandbox scratch must lie OUTSIDE git rev-parse --show-toplevel (codex C5: this repo IS .claude — repo-internal fallback would pollute on cleanup failure)

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh (prior cycle hook — read-only reference for AC4 fixture; NOT modified)
hooks/session-tmpfs-banner.sh (prior cycle hook — read-only reference; NOT modified)

/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (prior cycle out-of-repo deliverable — referenced by sandbox-destructive.sh AC6 fixture and by tmp-cleanup-install.sh S1 manifest; system-file itself NOT modified this cycle)

Codex C1: /close --force and /commit --force/--bulk bypass the shippability gate. Documented as audited overrides. Future cycle to evaluate enforcement on force paths.

Codex C7: User requirement says 'Reads use latest entry; writes use CAS'. score-inject.sh and other existing readers consume agent-scores.json directly. Reconciled: scores.json authoritative-for-reads; lifecycle log is audit-trail-only. AC9/AC10/AC11 satisfied. Future cycle if user wants log-as-source-of-truth (projection script).

OBJ-4.resolution: AC-H3 split into AC-H3a (subagent_type absent + legacy resolves single valid in_progress idx with subagent_call metadata → Case B writes; preserves AC-H2 serial dispatch) and AC-H3b (4 enumerated no-write orphan cases: subagent_type absent + legacy None; subagent_type non-empty + typed miss; subagent_type non-empty + no in_progress; subagent_type non-empty + multi-in_progress). Removes iter1 contradiction.

OBJ-5.resolution: Helper _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step rewritten: removed iter1 else: start=0 fallback. New behavior: ip_index None → return None immediately (caller exits 0 no-write per AC-H3b). Added MANDATORY multi-in_progress guard at _current_in_progress_index entry. Embedded concrete Python reference implementation in Technical Hints. Added OBJ-5 bullet in Edge Cases.

OBJ-6.resolution: Per dispatch prompt: acceptance_criteria_path=null and blast_radius_map_path=null; embedded ticket ACs (10 ACs in ticket-DH.md) are authoritative. Codex iter3 raised BLOCKER ISSUE-1: complexity_tier=STANDARD triggers /dev Step 8 test-writer which expects non-null AC JSON path. BA elected to follow dispatch prompt instruction and surfaced trade-off here for orchestrator decision.

development_approach.strategy: D: align PreTool literal match to PostTool exact-only by adding exact_only enum branch + switching read_grant's call. H: replace bookmark in_progress inference with bounded-window K=3 first-unflipped-match keyed on stdin.tool_input.subagent_type, gated on a single valid in_progress anchor (no canonical[0:3] fallback when ip_index is None or multi). Mutually-exclusive Case A (subagent_type non-empty) / Case B (subagent_type absent). None-key guard before _record_subagent_call_legacy. Multi-in_progress guard at _current_in_progress_index entry.
development_approach.validation_approach: Static: pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py (existing + new). Smoke: invoke each of 6 production PreTool call sites once with exact + once with substring (AC-D3). For H: simulated parallel/serial/orphan dispatch via direct Python test invocation of posttool-subagent-track.py.

codex_consult_iter3.feedback_summary: iter3 codex via no-tee channel returned clean CODEX_FEEDBACK. (a) AC-H3a composes correctly with multi-in_progress guard — multi-anchor routes to AC-H3b no-write (PASS). (b) AC-H3b sub-case (3) + helper ip_index None → None forms closed loop with no canonical[0:3] escape (PASS). Codex raised ONE in_scope_real_bug not on QA iter2's list: ISSUE-1 BLOCKER — acceptance_criteria_path=null combined with complexity_tier=STANDARD violates /dev Step 8 test-writer contract.

findings.rationale: Codex verified composition. Multi-in_progress returns None from guarded _current_in_progress_index → routes to AC-H3b no-write (sub-case 4), not AC-H3a. No contradiction.
Codex verified closed loop. ip_index is None → helper returns None before start is computed → Case A exits 0 no-write → no fallthrough → no canonical[0:3] scan.

findings.issue: ISSUE-1 (Codex BLOCKER): acceptance_criteria_path=null + complexity_tier=STANDARD creates downstream-pipeline contract gap. /dev Step 8 test-writer gate fires when tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high; test-writer expects non-null AC JSON path.
findings.proposed_fix: Two acceptable resolutions: (R1) Regenerate docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json from the 10 D+H ACs in ticket markdown. (R2) Downgrade complexity_tier to SMALL — SMALL allows skipping companion JSONs and matches D+H actual scope (3 files, ≤200 LOC).
findings.rationale: Codex finding is correct and traceable to commands/dev.md Step 8 gate. Dispatch prompt for iter3 explicitly instructed null + _companion_artifacts_note. Per BA contract 'Analytical Authority': treat orchestrator instructions as authoritative for routing/scoping. BA followed dispatch and surfaced finding here so QA iter3 / orchestrator can choose R1 or R2.
